title: About the Commodity Price Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Commodity Price Survey
21 September 2023, 10:45am
Information about taking part in the Commodity Price Survey, such as why we carry out the survey, how we selected you, and how to complete the questionnaire.
We carry out this survey to collect information on commodity prices over time. The comparative change or movement in commodity prices is used to calculate business price indexes.
Businesses across all sectors of the New Zealand economy take part in this survey.
Any business can be selected to participate – we select approximately 2,200. Your business may have been selected because:
- all businesses in the industry are ordered by total revenue (size), and we select a representative from the industry to represent each grouping of similarly sized businesses
- your business is a representative of other businesses producing or providing the same commodity, or unique in producing or providing a commodity different to that of other, similar businesses.
To accurately track the change in prices, we need to survey same participants each quarter.
Unfortunately, we can’t tell you exactly how long your business will be included in the survey sample for. This is because reselection of survey participants for any particular industry varies according to how much change occurs within that industry.
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Commodity Price Survey.
The survey is carried out every quarter – in February, May, August, and November.

## How to complete the survey
You can complete the survey questionnaire by filling in a paper or electronic (Excel) form.
- Contact us for survey help 26 May 2020
- Business survey information 31 July 2017
- Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data 20 May 2026
- Business 01 January 2017 Image: work table meeting
